name: Feeding America
mission: Our mission is to advance change in America by ensuring fair access to nutritious food for all in partnership with food banks, policymakers, supporters, and the communities we serve.
theoryOfChange: In partnership with subject matter experts, practitioners, and member food banks, Feeding America evaluates programs, interventions, and strategies that address food insecurity and diet-related chronic disease. By leveraging the strength of the food bank network, Feeding America has the opportunity to disseminate best practices that facilitate continuous learning and improvement of programs and build evidence of improved life outcomes for the people we serve.

scoreSummary: Feeding AmericaHQ shows strong impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Accredited Charity (Better Business Bureau (BBB) Wise Giving Alliance)
Top Charity (Ranked #1 on Top 100 Charities list) (Forbes)
Highest-scoring proposal in 100&Change competition (John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation)
Partnership (U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS))

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 97% of reported revenue appears to be phantom noncash contributions
keyConcerns.detail: At this level, reported program ratios are likely meaningless — inflated by noncash valuations rather than actual cash spending on programs.

components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 28/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Build reserves toward your board-approved target range (often 3-12 months), adjusted for revenue volatility and obligations.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: Website shows related EIN (36-2994412), likely parent/subsidiary of 36-3673599
Evaluation source domain 'vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com' is not a known trusted evaluator
Strengths are generic — could apply to any charity
Strengths are generic — could apply to any charity

[baseline vs zakat] The Zakat lens explicitly states the charity is not recommended for Zakat, while the Baseline lens implies it is suitable for 'sadaqah-eligible giving'. While Sadaqah is different from Zakat, the phrasing in Baseline could be misinterpreted by a user looking for Zakat-eligible charities.

[baseline vs strategic] The Strategic lens narrative suggests the charity 'focuses more on immediate help than on fixing the reasons why people are hungry', and that it 'treats the symptoms of hunger without necessarily fixing the root causes of poverty'. However, the Baseline lens mentions 'policy and advocacy' and 'policy change' as part of its work, which could be interpreted as addressing root causes.

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: GIK inflation: 97% of revenue is noncash contributions

risks.description: GIK inflation: 97% of revenue is noncash contributions

score_details.score_summary: Feeding AmericaHQ shows strong impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://www.feedingamerica.org/ways-to-give)

all_citations.claim: The charity has a 96.0/100 score and high financial accountability.
The organization reported total revenue of $5,047,685,550.
The organization distributes pounds of food through its network.
The charity provides food products and grants to member food banks.
The charity offers various ways to give, suitable for general charitable donations.
The organization highlights its program outcomes and financial highlights annually.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The charity shows strong financial health and efficiency in its spending, though its focus on policy and advocacy creates a different donor fit than traditional direct-aid groups.
areas_for_improvement: Limited working capital of only 1.4 months
Heavy focus on advocacy may not appeal to all direct-service donors

dimension_explanations.alignment: This organization is a good fit for sadaqah-eligible giving and donors interested in large-scale food security and policy change [12]. While it has a long track record of service, its primary focus on advocacy and domestic policy may differ from groups that focus only on direct international relief [14].
dimension_explanations.impact: Feeding AmericaHQ directs 98.3% of its total revenue toward its programs, which include food distribution and policy work [1][2]. The organization has a high Charity Navigator score of 96.0/100, reflecting strong accountability and financial health [1]. It moves millions of pounds of food through its network to help families facing hunger [3][5].

baseline_narrative.headline: Feeding AmericaHQ works to end hunger by distributing food and advocating for policy changes across the United States.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 98.3% ensures most funds reach those in need
Excellent fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.01 to raise every $1
Strong external rating of 96.96.0/100 from Charity Navigator
baseline_narrative.summary: Feeding AmericaHQ manages a large network of food banks to ensure people have access to nutritious meals [3]. The organization reported a total revenue of $5,047,685,550 to support its nationwide hunger-relief efforts [2].

all_citations.quote: Grants and other assistance to domestic organizations

all_citations.claim: $4,627,410,422 received in noncash food and grocery contributions
all_citations.quote: Noncash food and grocery contributions received: 4627410422

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its massive operational scale and financial efficiency, balanced against a systemic impact model that results in a higher cost per beneficiary.

areas_for_improvement.context: The charity maintains only <cite id="1">1.4 months of working capital</cite>, which is lean for an organization of this size.
Approximately <cite id="5">94% of total revenue is derived from noncash contributions</cite>, creating a high sensitivity to supply chain shifts.

bbb_assessment.summary: Meets all standards for charity accountability with strong governance and financial oversight.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization mitigates this through a vast network of 714 domestic partners to distribute risk.
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="5">97% reliance on noncash contributions</cite>, which can inflate revenue figures compared to cash-only charities.

confidence.notes: Very high transparency and data availability.

known_gaps: High GIK percentage makes cash-only comparisons difficult

alignment.explanation: This charity serves as a strong option for sadaqah, focusing on <cite id="5">equitable food access</cite> for the poor and needy in the US.
alignment.improvement: The charity could improve alignment for Muslim donors by <cite id="5">clearly stating whether any programs meet zakat-eligibility criteria</cite> or adding a dedicated giving page for faith-based donors.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this institution due to its <cite id="1">96.96.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator rating</cite> and <cite id="1">100% independent board governance</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="1">improve its accountability beacon score</cite> from 89 toward a perfect 100 by enhancing public disclosure of specific internal policies.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly efficient with a <cite id="1">98.3% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="1">perfect 100 Impact beacon score</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">building reserves toward a 3-12 month target</cite> to ensure long-term stability during economic downturns.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Verified outcomes with documented theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Provides cash and in-kind support to a national network of food banks.

rich_narrative.headline: Feeding AmericaHQ leads a national network dedicated to advancing food equity and ensuring nutritious food access for all Americans.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support large-scale, highly efficient food infrastructure and advocacy within the United States.
donor_motivations: Value high-volume food rescue and logistics
Want to support domestic hunger relief at a national scale
Prefer organizations with top-tier financial efficiency ratings
Interested in systemic solutions to food inequity
giving_considerations: Donations support a network of food banks rather than one specific location
The organization is heavily reliant on noncash (GIK) donations
Focus is domestic (USA and Puerto Rico) only
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for international humanitarian relief operations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified outcomes and a documented theory of change, though cost-per-beneficiary is high due to the advocacy model.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Feeding America uses a hub-and-spoke model to rescue food at scale and distribute it through a national network of partner food banks.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the billions in food products rescued actually reach those in need effectively.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 10.0% growth rate and massive national demand, the organization has high capacity for additional cash funding.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by a <cite id="1">100.0% program ratio</cite> which significantly exceeds the <cite id="1">83.0% peer median</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar focus on humanitarian relief and advocacy
similar_organizations.name: IMRC

similar_organizations.differentiator: Smaller scale advocacy and relief focus
similar_organizations.name: Rebuilding Alliance

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar focus on systemic advocacy and legal change
similar_organizations.name: ACLU Foundation

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">98.3% program expense ratio</cite> and spends only <cite id="1">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
Feeding America is governed by an <cite id="1">independent board of 17 members</cite> and holds a <cite id="1">96.33/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>.
They successfully processed over <cite id="5">$4.6 billion in noncash food contributions</cite> to support domestic food banks.

strengths_deep_dive: Feeding America's strength lies in its role as a national logistics backbone. By securing <cite id="5">$4.8 billion in grants and similar amounts paid</cite>, they empower local food banks to serve their specific communities. Their financial health is underscored by a <cite id="1">perfect 100 score in the Impact beacon</cite> from Charity Navigator, reflecting a highly effective model for large-scale resource mobilization. Furthermore, their <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> ensures strong accountability and conflict-of-interest management while overseeing nearly <cite id="2">$5 billion in annual resources</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Feeding America operates as the nation's largest domestic hunger-relief organization, functioning as a central hub for a network of food banks and partner agencies. The organization manages a massive logistics and advocacy operation, reporting <cite id="2">$4,916,912,461 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. Their primary mission involves <cite id="5">advancing equitable access to nutritious food</cite> through large-scale food rescue and disaster response. A defining characteristic of their financial model is the heavy reliance on in-kind donations, with <cite id="5">$4,627,410,422 received in noncash food and grocery contributions</cite>. This scale allows them to maintain a <cite id="1">98.3% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that nearly every dollar and donated item is directed toward hunger relief. Beyond immediate food distribution, the charity invests in <cite id="5">hunger research and systemic change</cite> to address the root causes of food insecurity across the United States and Puerto Rico. While they operate with high efficiency, their model focuses on <cite id="3">domestic grants and assistance</cite> to local organizations rather than direct individual service, distributing over <cite id="2">$682 million in cash grants</cite> to 714 recipients in 2023.
